chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. B.A. No.3242 of 2025 (Shhaid Ali vs. The State) Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Mr. Justice Syed Fiaz-ul-Hassan Shah For hearing of bail application Date of hearing & order 20.01.2026 Mr. Amjad Hussain Baloch, advocate for applicant Mr. Ali Haider Salim, Addl: PG Sindh O R D E R ----- Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro, J:- Applicant is seeking post arrest bail in a case bearing Crime No.199/2025, registered at Police Station Malir City, Karachi, u/s 93(a) CNS (Amendment) Act by means of this application. 2. Applicant was arrested on 09.04.2025 by a police team of Police Station Malir City, from Malir Court, Near Muhamadi School, Malir City, Karachi, and from him allegedly 1020 grams of charas was recovered. Hence, he was booked in the present case. 3. Learned counsel for the applicant has pleaded for bail on the ground that applicant is first offender; the case has been challenged and the applicant is no more required for further investigation. And that even requirement of Section 17 Sindh Control of Narcotics Substances Act, 2024 has not been done.. 4. Learned DPG has opposed bail but concedes that applicant is the first offender. Accordingly, as the applicant is first offender, the case has been challaned, and prima facie, the requirement of Section 17 Sindh Control of Narcotics Substances Act, 2024 has not been done. We find the case against the applicant to be of further inquiry and grant him bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/ (Rupees one hundred thousand only) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5.

Accordingly, as the applicant is first offender, the case has been challaned, and prima facie, the requirement of Section 17 Sindh Control of Narcotics Substances Act, 2024 has not been done. We find the case against the applicant to be of further inquiry and grant him bail subject to his furnishing a solvent surety in the sum of Rs.100,000/ (Rupees one hundred thousand only) with P.R. bond in the like amount to be executed to the satisfaction of the trial Court. 5. Bail application stands disposed of in the above terms. The observations made hereinabove are tentative in nature and would not prejudice case of either party at trial. JUDGE JUDGE Rafiq/PA.
